It occupies no visible width or height, does not affect spacing, and introduces no additional UI elements; the only spatial effect is that the main content region may appear after a brief load, filling the predefined two-column area at once.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered logic layer that occupies no layout space; it runs before visual components paint and does not scroll or stick.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is another non-visual component that operates in the background of the two-column layout. Conceptually, it sits between the ministry-list in the left sidebar and the ministry-card stack in the right column, synchronizing the selected ministry based on URL parameters or user clicks. It does not draw any UI elements, but its presence determines which item in the left list receives the highlighted state and which ministry card in the right column may be scrolled into view or visually emphasized. It has no intrinsic width, height, or padding and does not alter the physical structure of the grid; it only changes styling states and scroll behavior of already positioned components.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered controller that does not occupy space; it influences visual states and potential scroll-to behavior but is itself invisible and non-sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The ministry-list component forms the entire left sidebar column of the main content band. It begins directly under the page-level heading area (which spans both columns) and aligns flush to the left edge of the centered content container. Horizontally, it occupies about 25–30% of the container width. Vertically, it stretches with the content, starting near the top of the content band and extending downward as needed. Inside, ministries are displayed as a dense vertical list: each item spans the full width of this sidebar, with approximately 12–16px of vertical padding and 12–16px of left and right padding, separated by thin horizontal rules. The currently selected ministry item is visually highlighted with a slightly darker or tinted background and perhaps a bold label, but retains the same item height to preserve the dense rhythm. There is about 24px of space between any introductory sidebar label (e.g., 'Ministries') and the first list item, and about 24–32px gap between the bottom of the list and any footer content. On narrow screens, this sidebar collapses above the main ministry cards, spanning 100% width as a full-width list while maintaining the same vertical item spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the document as a standard left column; it scrolls naturally with the page, without sticky or floating behavior. On smaller viewports, it reflows to stack above the main column, becoming a full-width block.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The ministry-card is the primary unit in the right main column. All ministry-card instances are stacked vertically, forming a single reading column that begins just below the page title and introductory text, aligned to the right of the ministry-list sidebar. The right column takes about 70–75% of the centered container width, with the cards each spanning nearly the full width of this column (leaving only the standard container padding on the far left and right). Each card has a flat, minimalist presentation: a subtle border or bottom rule may delineate it, but there are no heavy shadows. Internally, a card has 16–24px of padding on all sides. At the very top-left of the card is the ministry title set as a prominent heading, with extra top padding of around 8–12px before any content above it. On the same horizontal line, in the top-right corner of the card, sits the ministry-share-link icon or text, aligned baseline with the title and inset about 16px from the right edge. Below the title, there is approximately 8–16px of vertical space before the descriptive text begins. The descriptive and contact information content flows in a simple, left-aligned paragraph structure, with line spacing mirroring the utilitarian feel of the site. There is about 16–24px of vertical space between the end of the descriptive content and the row of action buttons managed by the ministry-card-actions-router. The bottom of each card ends with roughly 16–24px of padding. Cards are separated from each other by about 24–32px of vertical space, ensuring a clear but not overly airy separation. On smaller screens, each card still behaves as a full-width block, maintaining similar internal padding but potentially allowing the share link and actions row to wrap under the title.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level elements flowing vertically in the main column; they scroll with the page and do not float. Within the right column, cards expand to fill available width while respecting container padding and responsive breakpoints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Within each ministry-card, the ministry-card-actions-router controls the placement and behavior of the key action controls (e.g., Give, Serve, View Events). Visually, this appears as a horizontal row of 2–4 buttons or links aligned to the left, positioned in the lower portion of the card. The action row sits beneath the body and contact text, with about 16–24px of vertical space separating it from the text above. The buttons themselves follow the flat, utilitarian style: they are medium-sized, with around 8–12px of vertical padding and 16–24px of horizontal padding, separated from each other by about 8–12px of horizontal space. The action row does not span the full width of the card; instead, it typically occupies the left 60–70% of the card’s width, leaving empty space on the right for a clean, uncluttered feel. There is approximately 16px of padding between the action row and the bottom edge of the card. On narrow screens, these buttons may wrap into two lines but remain grouped closely with consistent vertical spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This component is anchored inside each ministry-card as a horizontal control strip. It flows with the card’s content and scrolls normally; it is not sticky or floating.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The ministry-share-link is a small, subtle utility element placed inside each ministry-card. It is positioned in the card’s header region, aligned with the ministry title. Horizontally, it sits in the top-right corner of the card’s content area, offset inward by roughly 16px from the right edge and aligned to the same baseline as the title on the left. Its visual footprint is modest—a small icon or a short text link like 'Share'—so it does not compete with the ministry name for attention. There is at least 8px of space between the share link and the card’s top padding to prevent crowding. The positioning ensures that as the card widens or narrows, the link remains right-aligned within the card header. On smaller screens where horizontal space is limited, the share link may drop to a new line just below the title but remains within the top 20–25% of the card height so it still reads as part of the header.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed relative to the top-right corner of each ministry-card’s inner content area; it scrolls with the card and reflows right-aligned or onto a second line as the card’s width changes, but does not stick to the viewport.
